sentences.text: Hi, Kaylee.
How are you?
I'm doing well, can you hear me?
I can, yeah.
How are you?
Sorry, let me close my door real quick.
We're good.
We always use Microsoft Teams, and I swear, every time I try to get in Zoom, it makes me, like, reset up my audio for some reason, so…
It's so funny, that's very consistent with what I hear, to be honest with you.
Everybody that uses Teams, anytime they log into one of our meetings, it's… So we'd update Zoom, or, like, whatever it might be, yes.
Okay, so this shouldn't take too long.
Is Jennifer planning on joining as well?
I just sent her the invite, I'm not sure if she is, or she might hop on a little bit late.
She has the meeting notes in here, so I didn't know if she was going to, but this should be fairly quick.

I mean, basically, I just want to make sure that you're able to log in without any issues, and then just walk you through a couple functionalities, make sure you know how to use the tool, and then that's it.
So, that's really it.
Did you… you should… Go ahead, I'm sorry.
I am logged in, I got the invite.
Oh, fabulous.
Perfect.
So when you log in, you'll see your portfolio here.
Which of… which properties are your market rate versus which ones are the affordable ones?
The market rate ones are 320 at the vibe.
And… that's it.
I guess the others are, like.
Some of them are partially affordable, partially market rate.
Okay.
But 320 at the Vibe is the only, like, fully… Market rate property.
Okay, cool.
So I'll just hop in on any one of these, but a couple things on this page.
So, obviously, these are your properties.

I don't have research set up, we typically don't set that up, but that's more so geared towards, acquisitions and new development all week, because Jennifer's actually about to enter, so I'll hold off on anything.
So while she's joining, is there a reason all of those have market in the name?
No, it just means, like, it's the market, so it's this is…
Oh, okay.
This is the market for Bridget Avery Ranch.
That's how our account is set up, too.
Yeah, so all… every single time this is set up, it's set up with market in the name, because it's that specific market that we're looking at.
Okay, that makes sense.
Hi, Jennifer.
Hi, how are you?
I'm good, how are you?
Good!
So, I was just telling Kaylee, we're gonna, we're just gonna use this call to, one, ensure that you're able to log into the tool, and then…
I just logged in.
Fabulous.
already looking at everything.
It's pretty cool, I love it.
Good.

And then I'm basically just gonna show you some basic functionalities of the tool, and then… at the end, we'll decide, like, kind of when you guys want to have a check-in, how long you guys need to use the tool for.
So, this'll be quick, quick and easy.
So you'll have all of your properties here, as I was just explaining, so you have research here.
This is not currently turned on.
Refresh my memory, do you guys do anything with, acquisitions or new development?
No.
Okay, and then…
And we do do new development, but we're not in acquisitions.
Okay, so if you guys came on board, if your development team wanted seats for the research aspect, to spin up a market survey for a development project, we can enable that for you.
But you do have Explore turned on, so this is going to give you that high-level market-sub-market data, so you can type in any city, neighborhood, zip code, you could type in a property address.
And then, what I would suggest is if, when you're in here, this is just gonna give you that high-level market look.
As it's loading.
Utilize these filters at the top, so I'm up in New Hampshire, so the Boston neighborhood.
You can utilize the filters to kind of hone in on what's more relevant to you all, just in regards to, like.
vintage, how many units are in the property, and that will, like, filter down what you're actually looking at, so it'll make… it'll make it more relevant to you all.
So that's what Explore is.
And then, if there's additional users that you guys want to utilize the tool, the platform to test it out.
Over here, manage users, you would just invite the new user, type in their email address, select their user role, and then when you select these, it will tell you what their permissions are.
Right now, you guys both have admin access.
That means that you can add users, edit comp sets, all that kind of stuff.
But obviously, read-only, you can only log in and not make any changes at all, so you can add additional users if you guys want others to see the tool and kind of use it.
So To, view the analytics, you just click on the property.
A couple things to point out.
So you do have different filtering options here, so if you want to look at the information based off of just a couple of comps, you can.
And then you can look at different, like, bedrooms, bathrooms, square footage, all that kind of stuff.
One thing I do want to highlight is customized metrics.
You do want to… toggle this, and go through and apply any metrics that you want to see in your view.
This is user-specific, so Kaylee, whatever you have toggled on will not affect Jennifer's view, and vice versa.

So I would just go through, select the metrics that you want to see, uncheck the ones that you don't care about, and then apply customized view to all market surveys, and that will do it for all 5 properties, so you only have to do this once.
Okay?
And then when you hit apply, it'll… Show you all that information here.
There we go.
Right now, we're looking at the information as of live yesterday, but you can aggregate the data however you want.
You have full control over your comps, so up here in the top right, edit comps.
Here is the map view, your property's purple, the properties currently in your COMSA are the white pins, and then these are all other properties in the area.
You can add them directly from this view just by adding comp, or over on the left-hand side, you get a list view of that same information.
So your property here at the top, comp's currently in the comp set.
If you don't want them included, you can X them off.
And then down below, there is a list of all the other properties in the area.
If you are not finding a comp that you want to add, you can add comp here, type in the name or address.
find it in the list.
For whatever reason, if you don't find it in the list, you can reach out to me and I can have our team at it.
But then again, there's different filtering options at the top here.
That's how you would edit.
Your comps?
You can have reports scheduled, or just download them ad hoc, so you would just type in your email address, download now, and it would download the information.
Just know that for the Excel exports, whatever you have turned on in this view.
is what's gonna transfer over to the Excel, so if you don't have certain metrics toggled on.
those are not going to reflect.
So, whatever you see here in-app is what you'll see in the Excel download.
So you have both PDF and Excel.
And then these are the different reports at the top, so you have, obviously, market survey, you can scroll all the way down, pricing and availability by floor plan.
We have your fees and deposits.
What if you notice a discrepancy on the fees section?

So, you can either suggest an update, but what's going to be coming soon, and I believe in the next few weeks, in the fees and deposits, as well as the in-unit and community amenities, you're gonna be able to edit this section.
So, no other section in here will be editable, just because it's all public data, but in regards to the fees and deposits and amenities, in the very near future.
You can edit if you find a discrepancy, otherwise you can suggest update.
So you can say, okay, so this is for… our property…
Yeah.
It's the admin fee.
It's the admin anymore.
Admin fee, current, suggested update, so you don't have one?
Correct.
We used to, but now we don't.
no longer have an admin fee.
And then you would submit.
Okay?
Or you could email me, and I could… would do the exact same thing, so whatever your preference is.
Okay.
So that's here.
Floor plans, you can toggle between All of the, the average of all of the studios or one-bedrooms, or you can look at the roll… or the breakdown, so every unique floor plan that we have.
Yeah, it also says for Bridge at Avery Ranch that we have a brief restriction, and we do not.
Got the free prescription.
For dogs.
Okay, so that would be another suggestion update.
Okay.
Perfect.
So I can do that, or you can do that, so…
Read restrictions.

I actually want to just, so, some of the… so, one thing, actually, one of our partners found is some of the information that we're pulling, whether it's from their PMS or if you guys list on Apartments.
com, sometimes this information's being pulled from Apartments.
com, because they do a really nice job with, like, fees and deposits and amenities.
I'll go check there to see if it's there, but I can, suggest the update and then just remove the breed restrictions in regards to the dogs.
Okay.
Yeah, let me look at our listing real quick.
It definitely should not say that.
I… don't see it anywhere.
I don't see it there either, so maybe we gather… we grabbed that from… somewhere else, but yes, I can submit… that update.
Okay.
Bridget Avery…
Pet restrictions… -Oh.
Feed restrictions.
And that's usually updated, it's… so it's… it's someone manual who's actually doing that, so they'll go into the system on the back end, and they'll remove that or update it to whatever it might be.
So that will probably take 24 hours for somebody to go into the system.
Ken?
Okay, so limited data tag, here's a good one.
So, if you hover over this, so this one, we're flagging whether or not we think somebody's hiding units.

Basically, we're just letting you know, like, hey, there's We're potentially hiding units, so we're gonna show you what we're capturing, but just take that with a grain of salt, so you can unhide this, and that… you only have to do that once.
But essentially, we do that a lot of times for lease-up properties and things like that, because typically, lease-ups aren't revealing all of their availability at once.
So here you'll see what we're capturing, but basically, we're like, take it with a grain of salt, they might be hiding additional units.
So that, when you see that, that's what that means, but you can click to unhide the data.
Okay.
I'm trying to think if there's anything else.
If you click into any of these, so any of these metrics, you can click into.
So, for instance, we're showing that you guys have 32 units available, you click into that, these are the 32 that we are showing as available.
You can do that for any of your comps as well.
Okay.

And then same at the top, if you click into any of these property cards, we're gonna give you a link to just the Google Map, show who's currently managing them, how many units, when it was built, a link to the, website, so it just gives you kind of a little information on who's managing or… Just information on the property itself.
Do you guys have any questions for me while we're here?
So, what is the difference between the Explore section and the Research section?
Because I know you don't have research set up, but I was thinking about it, because we do have our development team.
Yep.
That might find that useful.

So, research allows you to… for an acquisitions purpose, which you guys don't do, you would type in the name of a property that you guys don't currently own or manage, find it in the list, and essentially this is just for acquisitions.
This would be specific to, like.
acquiring, obviously, so you… it would spin up a market survey for a current property that you're looking to take over.
Otherwise, for new development, you could say, new report, create a custom property.

And they would be able to type in if they have the land already purchased, like, the crossroads, when they're targeting that the property's gonna be complete, how many units, and then they can add in Kind of what their unit type mix will look like.

And then they would say, save property and generate comps, and our system would automatically pull in the 9 best comps that were saying, based on the information entered in, here's some data on the available… the comps in the area.
So, it essentially just spins… spins up a market survey.
For a new development project.
So, are we able to test that out, or no?
If you have somebody on the development side, I mean, I'd like to meet with them first, just so they understand the platform and all that kind of stuff.
Do you guys work with, specifically with the development team?
So, realistically, in a trial, we don't typically turn this on unless it's, like, somebody who's actually on the device.
Yeah, we, Jamie, just to give you a little insight, we're, we work for Journeyman Construction, so we construct, develop, and manage.
Okay.
So, everything is all in-house here, so…
So, if you have specific users, one, usually it makes the most sense for us to meet with them so we can actually walk them through the tool versus them trying to navigate this blindly.
But if they're busy and you want them to log in and figure it out themselves, this is a permission, so even if I turn this on, you two necessarily wouldn't have permissions for it.
It's user-specific.
So, for my properties in Explorer, that's unlimited users.
that's whoever.
But this tool right here, I actually have to, like, give an email address and have it activated for somebody.
If you have an email address, I can have it… I can add them to the account and have it turned on for them.

I do, and I… the only reason I'm pushing for this is because we just signed up for, you know, we have Yardi Matrix, we have RealPage, but I mentioned this product, and they were interested in testing it out to see if it was better.
Okay.
Do you use it.

So I can't actually… I have to have our team actually turn on… like, research isn't even turned on right now, so I have to have our team turn it on, so if you want to follow the call, email me who should have access.
Once our team turns this on, I'll add them to the account, and I will enable that.
Because right here, it usually says, like, enable research, and I would have to turn on a button for those people.
Gotcha.
Yeah.
Okay, I will do that, because that could be very useful for us, and I mean, depending on how well it works, we could maybe switch over to this product, so…
This is… so this is what that looks like.
So right here, there's a research button.
And I would have to enable research for those users.
And there's, like, a limited number of seats, it's not just unlimited, so… That's what that is.
Okay.
Definitely.
Yeah, I can have that.
How many, how many users do you want to have access to that?
Just two.
Okay.
And if there comes a time that they want to meet with us, just so we can walk them through the tool so they understand it, that would probably make sense as well.
Okay.
Do you guys have any other questions?
I don't.
Okay.
How long, I guess just from an evaluation standpoint, how long do you all think that you'll need access before making a decision?
I don't know, Jennifer, what do you think?

I mean, I would say at least 2 weeks, that way we can, Because we do our market surveys monthly, or sorry, weekly, so it would be nice to have access for 2 weeks so that we could pull You know, a report for 2 weeks, and kind of… compare and… All of that, so…
How about this?
Why don't we set a tentative call in 2 weeks from now?
Okay.
I'll play… I can leave it live past that, but we'll use it as a check-in, see how things are going, see what your feedback has been, answer any questions you guys have.
And if you feel like you still need more time, we can revisit at that point, but that at least gives us a time to check back in with you.
Just to see how things are going.
With that being said, if questions or concerns come up in the meantime, do not wait the two weeks until I talk to you.

Like, feel free to reach out at any point, like, hey, this doesn't look right, or hey, were you gathering this metric, or… hey, we don't have breed restrictions, how do I go ahead and update that, or can you update it?
We can do that, but… Do you want to schedule a call for 2 weeks from now, just to hop on and reconnect, just to see how your trial's going?
Sure.
Yeah.
That would be great.
Okay, would the same time in 2 weeks, so same time on the 22nd, would that work for you both?
Let me look real quick.
On the 22nd?
Monday the 22nd, yes.
Yeah, that should be fine.
Expired for me as well.
Perfect.
And if there's anyone else that you want a demo of the tool, by all means, let me know.
I'd be happy to set that up, but again, feel free to add whoever you want to the… to the trial account.
They can play around with it.
But yeah, I will, I'll send over a calendar invite for the 22nd, same time.
And we will go from there.
But again, if anything comes up, let me know.
I will add, Kaylee, I just got your email.
I will have the research turned on, and I will add both Allison and Nick to the account as well.
Okay.
Awesome.
Perfect.
Thank you so much for that.
Of course, yeah, let me know if anything comes up, but I'll, plan on talking to you guys in a couple of weeks.
Awesome, thank you.
Sounds great, have a great day.
You too.
Bye.
